skills: arithmetic word problems in English (fractions of a group, totals)
vocabulary in context (guessing word meaning from context, e.g. 'breeze')
question_types: situational response selection (what to say given a scenario)
single sentence with tense/grammar correction (had just left when...)
data table with 3-4 linked questions comparing quantities
diary/personal recount passage with 4-5 linked questions (topic, time, location/origin, vocabulary-in-context)
dialogue fill-in-the-blank across multiple settings (school, phone, travel)
reading_patterns: first-person diary/travel recount (~100-130 words) followed by questions on topic summary, inferred timing, inferred origin/hometown, and in-context vocabulary meaning
comparative data table (animal speeds, etc.) followed by 3-4 questions requiring ranking/comparison across multiple rows
dialogue_patterns: sympathy/well-wishing short exchange ('Get well soon' type)
travel/experience recount exchange (how did you get there / what did you do)
distractor_patterns: correct holiday but wrong associated phrase (mismatched greeting for the occasion)
table-reading distractor: comparison direction reversed (faster/slower swapped)
arithmetic distractor: forgetting to take the fraction of the total (whole number instead of half)
vocabulary-in-context distractor: a literal/unrelated dictionary meaning instead of the passage-supported meaning
near-miss grammatical tense distractor for past perfect items
answer_logic: data table items require reading across 2+ rows/columns and applying a comparative adjective correctly
diary/recount items require inferring unstated facts (hometown, start time) from contextual clues (time zone, description of scenery, etc.)
vocabulary-in-context items are answered by nearby sentence support, not by general vocabulary knowledge alone
arithmetic items require correct operation order (find half of total before comparing to another given number)
notes: Extracted from native embedded PDF text via pdftotext. Slightly higher difficulty than 2558-2560: introduces past perfect, table+arithmetic combined items, and in-context vocabulary inference.
